segments.speaker: FATHER
segments.en: And are the classes on a Monday, too?

segments.speaker: FATHER
segments.en: Sorry?

segments.speaker: FATHER
segments.en: And that's in Grasford, isn't it?
segments.zh: 那是在格拉斯福德，对吧？

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Yes, up past the station.

segments.speaker: FATHER
segments.en: And will I have any parking problems there?
segments.zh: 那我在那儿停车会有问题吗？

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: No, there's always plenty available.
segments.zh: 不会，车位总是很充足。那您现在要给莉迪亚和卡莉报名吗？

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: So would you like to enrol Lydia and Carly now?

segments.speaker: FATHER
segments.en: OK.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: So can I have your full name ...

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to Stevenson's, one of the country's major manufacturers of metal goods.
segments.zh: 大家早上好，欢迎来到史蒂文森公司，这是国内主要的金属制品制造商之一。感谢你们选择我们进行为期两周的工作体验。我叫朱莉娅·西蒙斯，从今年年初开始担任总经理。

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Thank you for choosing us for your two weeks of work experience.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: My name is Julia Simmons, and since the beginning of this year I've been the managing director.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Stevenson's is quite an old company.
segments.zh: 史蒂文森是一家相当老牌的公司。和我一样，创始人罗纳德·史蒂文森在离开学校后进入了钢铁行业——那是在1923年。他在1926年完成学徒期后创办了这家公司，尽管他实际上在两年之前，也就是1924年就开始制定计划了。他是一个非常有决心的年轻人！

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Like me, the founder, Ronald Stevenson, went into the steel industry when he left school – that was in 1923.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: He set up this company when he finished his apprenticeship, in 1926, although he actually started making plans two years earlier, in 1924.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: He was a very determined young man!

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Stevenson's long-term plan was to manufacture components for the machine tools industry – although in fact that never came about – and for the automotive industry, that is, cars and lorries.
segments.zh: 史蒂文森的长期计划是为机床行业制造零部件——尽管实际上这从未实现——以及为汽车行业，也就是汽车和卡车制造零部件。然而，这推迟了五年才发生，因为就在公司即将投产前不久，史蒂文森获得了为医院和医疗保健行业的其他参与者制造产品的机会，所以我们在头五年里做的就是这些。

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: However, there was a delay of five years before that happened, because shortly before the company went into production, Stevenson was given the opportunity to make goods for hospitals and other players in the healthcare industry, so that's what we did for the first five years.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Over the years, we've expanded the premises considerably – we were lucky that the site is big enough, so moving to a new location has never been necessary.
segments.zh: 多年来，我们大幅扩建了厂房——幸运的是场地足够大，所以从未需要搬迁。然而，现有的布局对于现代机械和生产方法来说远非理想，因此我们计划在未来五年内对这里进行大规模翻新。

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: However, the layout is far from ideal for modern machinery and production methods, so we intend to carry out major refurbishment of this site over the next five years.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: I'd better give you some idea of what you'll be doing during your two weeks with us, so you know what to expect.
segments.zh: 我最好让你们了解一下这两周你们会做些什么，这样你们就知道该期待什么了。大多数上午，你们会听一位经理的演讲，了解他们的部门，今天上午从研发部开始。你们每个人都会在每个部门待一段时间，观察工作进展并与员工交流——只要你们不彻底妨碍他们工作就行！过去，你们学校的一位老师会在每周结束时来了解小组的进展，但今年你们学校无法安排这一点。

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: Most mornings you'll have a presentation from one of the managers, to learn about their department, starting this morning with research and development.

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: And you'll all spend some time in each department, observing what's going on and talking to people – as long as you don't stop them from doing their work altogether!

segments.speaker: SARAH
segments.en: In the past, a teacher from your school has come in at the end of each week to find out how the group were getting on, but your school isn't able to arrange that this year.

pairs.note: founded 和 set up 都表示'建立'，是近义替换。
was founded 是被动语态，set up 是主动形式，但意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问公司成立时间，定位句提到 'He set up this company when he finished his apprenticeship, in 1926'，所以答案对应 1926 年。

pairs.note: Originally 对应 long-term plan，表示最初的计划。
manufactured goods for 被替换为 make goods for，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问最初Stevenson为谁生产商品，定位句提到long-term plan是制造零部件，但实际最初五年是为医疗行业生产，所以答案选A（医院等医疗行业）。

items.prompt: What does the speaker say about the company premises?

pairs.note: 题干中的'company premises'对应原文中的'the premises'，指同一地点。

pairs.q: what does the speaker say about
pairs.p: the layout is far from ideal
pairs.note: 题干问'说了什么'，原文用'layout is far from ideal'描述现状，是直接评价。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'the layout is far from ideal for modern machinery and production methods'，直接说明对厂房的评价，因此选B。

items.prompt: The programme for the work experience group includes

pairs.p: some idea of what you'll be doing
pairs.note: programme 被解释为 'some idea of what you'll be doing'，即活动安排。
work experience group 指的就是 'you'，即听众所在的实习小组。
includes 对应 'Most mornings you'll have'，表示包含的活动。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头说 'give you some idea of what you'll be doing'，直接对应题干 'programme'，后面具体描述活动，如 'Most mornings you'll have a presentation'，因此答案 C 正确。
